name: Tardelcuende — circular semigravel from Almazan
region_intro: Touring cycling around Almazán offers diverse terrain across its countryside landscapes, providing routes for various skill levels. The region is characterized by its varied topography, including areas along the Duero River, which features prominently in many routes. Cyclists can experience both flatter sections and moderate elevation gains, with the highest points reaching around 290 meters above sea level. The area provides a mix of paved and semi-gravel paths, suitable for different types of touring bicycles. Best touring cycling routes around Almazán The most popular touring cycling route is Nepas — circular from Almazan, a 26.9 miles (43.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete, offering a moderate ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Tardelcuende — circular semigravel from Almazan, a moderate 24.1 miles (38.7 km) path that includes semi-gravel sections and views of the rural landscape. Local touring cyclists also love the Plaza Mayor of Almazán – Plaza Mayor Viewpoint loop from Almazán, a 6.8 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through the town's historical center and to a viewpoint over the Duero River, often completed in about 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Almazán is defined by countryside landscapes, Duero River views, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging rides with significant elevation. The routes in Almazán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 22 reviews. More than 76 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almazán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almazán?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes documented around Almazán on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is well-suited for exploring by bike, with many community-created and tracked paths.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Almazán?
A: The terrain around Almazán is diverse, characterized by countryside landscapes. You'll find a mix of paved roads and semi-gravel paths, suitable for various touring bicycles. Many routes feature sections along or with views of the Duero River, and some extend into areas with moderate elevation gains, offering a good balance for different riding preferences.

Q: Are there options for different skill levels among the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Almazán caters to a range of abilities. The routes include easy loops perfect for casual riders or beginners, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Almazán – Plaza Mayor Viewpoint loop from Almazán&lt;/a&gt;, as well as moderate and more challenging rides with significant elevation gains for experienced cyclists. In total, there are 3 easy, 9 moderate, and 3 difficult routes.

Q: What historical or natural attractions can I explore by bike around Almazán?
A: Almazán and its surroundings are rich in attractions. You can cycle through the town's historical center to visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5555265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Almazán&lt;/a&gt; and its cantilevered viewpoint over the Duero River. Other historical sites accessible by bike include the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5519872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Herreros Gate&lt;/a&gt;. The routes often provide scenic views of the Duero River and the broader countryside.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy about the routes in Almazán?
A: The touring cycling routes in Almazán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from over 22 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic countryside, and the opportunities to explore both natural beauty and historical sites. Over 76 touring cyclists have used komoot to discover the area.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Almazán?
A: Yes, Almazán offers several circular touring cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041753855/nepas-circular-from-almazan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nepas — circular from Almazan&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 26.9-mile (43.3 km) trail. Another favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041705699/tardelcuende-circular-semigravel-from-almazan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardelcuende — circular semigravel from Almazan&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 24.1 miles (38.7 km) and includes semi-gravel sections.

Q: Can I find routes that include semi-gravel sections for touring cycling?
A: Absolutely. Almazán's touring cycling network includes routes with semi-gravel sections, providing a different kind of riding experience. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041705699/tardelcuende-circular-semigravel-from-almazan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tardelcuende — circular semigravel from Almazan&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 24.1-mile (38.7 km) path that allows you to enjoy the rural landscape on mixed surfaces.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for touring cycling routes in Almazán?
A: The routes vary significantly in length and time. You can find shorter, easy loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Almazán – Plaza Mayor Viewpoint loop from Almazán&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.8 miles (11.0 km) and takes about 54 minutes. Longer, moderate routes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041753855/nepas-circular-from-almazan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nepas — circular from Almazan&lt;/a&gt; extend to 26.9 miles (43.3 km) and typically take around 2 hours 35 minutes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Almazán offers several easy touring cycling routes that are great for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Almazán – Plaza Mayor Viewpoint loop from Almazán&lt;/a&gt; is a short and accessible 6.8-mile (11.0 km) route that takes you through the town's historical center. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31696717" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Almazán loop from Matamala de Almazán&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.7 miles (22.1 km) long.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Almazán?
A: The region around Almazán is generally pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. These seasons offer comfortable riding conditions and beautiful scenery. While summer can be warm, early mornings or late afternoons are still viable. Winter cycling is possible, but you should check local weather conditions as temperatures can be cooler.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Duero River?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Almazán incorporate the Duero River, which is a significant natural feature of the area. You can find routes that follow its course or offer scenic viewpoints, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Almazán – Plaza Mayor Viewpoint loop from Almazán&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to a viewpoint directly overlooking the river.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Almazán?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, many routes, especially those starting from Almazán itself, can be accessed from the town center. You can typically find parking in or around the main squares or public areas in Almazán and other villages that serve as starting points for the routes.
tips: Place with a picnic area in the shade, a small natural spring source and a resin hut to rest and protect from the rain. Nice resting place on an excursion.
Highest point between Tardelcuende and Almazán on the "white highway".
